facts.title: K-pop tops the world charts
facts.fact: K-pop has become a global force. In 2020 the group **BTS** (방탄소년단) reached **number one on the US Billboard Hot 100** with their first all-English single, a first for a Korean act.
facts.note: **방탄소년단** means "Bulletproof Boy Scouts"; fans worldwide call the group by its English name, BTS.

facts.title: The idol trainee system
facts.fact: Most K-pop idols spend years as **trainees** at an entertainment company, studying singing and dancing before they debut. Groups like **BLACKPINK** trained for years before their first release.
facts.note: 춤을 추다 = "to dance" (literally "to dance a dance"). Many idols are valued as much for dancing as for singing.

facts.title: Trot, an old genre made new
facts.fact: **Trot** (트로트) is one of Korea's oldest popular music styles, with a sentimental sound. TV audition shows in recent years sparked a huge revival, making it popular again with both old and young fans.
facts.note: **트로트** is loved across generations; TV singing contests gave it a big new audience.

facts.title: Pansori, a one-singer epic
facts.fact: **Pansori** (판소리) is a traditional form of musical storytelling in which a single singer performs a long tale, accompanied by one drummer. UNESCO recognized it as intangible cultural heritage in 2003.
facts.note: In **판소리**, the 소리 means "sound" or "voice"; the singer performs for hours with just a drum.

facts.title: Noraebang, the song room
facts.fact: A **noraebang** (노래방) is a private karaoke room rented by the hour. Singing together is a core social activity, often the last stop on a night out with friends or coworkers.
facts.korean_en: I go to a noraebang with friends.

facts.title: The gayageum, a string instrument
facts.fact: The **gayageum** (가야금) is a traditional Korean zither with twelve strings, plucked with the fingers. Its gentle sound has been played for well over a thousand years and still features in music today.
facts.note: **가야금** is one of the best-known 국악기 (traditional Korean instruments).

facts.title: Korean hip-hop goes mainstream
facts.fact: Korean **hip-hop** grew from an underground scene into a mainstream genre, helped by long-running TV rap competitions. Many rappers now top the charts and shape Korean pop music.
facts.note: **힙합** is borrowed from the English "hip-hop"; rap is often called **랩**.
